front: Work: resolves a metaphysical dispute about whether a man is 'going round' a squirrel by asking what it 'practically means'
back: Pragmatism: A New Name for Some Old Ways of Thinking (William James)
work: Pragmatism: A New Name for Some Old Ways of Thinking (William James)

front: Work: distinguishes between 'tender-minded' rationalists and 'tough-minded' empiricists in two columns of traits
work: Pragmatism: A New Name for Some Old Ways of Thinking (William James)

front: Work: claims ideas 'become true just in so far as they help us get into satisfactory relation with other parts of our experience' — truth as 'cash value'
work: Pragmatism: A New Name for Some Old Ways of Thinking (William James)

front: Work: reproduces the entirety of Walt Whitman's 'To You' and argues for a pluralistic rather than monistic reading
work: Pragmatism: A New Name for Some Old Ways of Thinking (William James)

front: Work: promotes 'epistemological behaviorism' and attacks representational theories that view knowledge as the 'mirror of nature'
back: Philosophy and the Mirror of Nature (Richard Rorty)

front: Philosopher: in Achieving Our Country, this neopragmatist favorably contrasts the agency of the progressive Left with the 'spectatorial' academic Left in the tradition of Foucault
Philosopher: this thinker's book defines 'ironists' who perpetually doubt their 'final vocabulary' of beliefs
Philosopher: this British foundherentist argued that Rorty did not really belong to pragmatism and wrote a play imagining a dialogue between Peirce and Rorty
Work: traced the origins of pragmatism through a discussion group at Harvard that included Peirce, James, and Oliver Wendell Holmes Jr.
